# Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

We as members, contributors, and maintainers pledge to make participation in
the Cauldron OS community a respectful and welcoming experience for everyone,
regardless of age, body size, disability, ethnicity, gender identity and
expression, level of experience, nationality, personal appearance, race,
religion, or sexual identity and orientation.

---

## Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contributes to a positive environment:

- Using welcoming and inclusive language
- Being respectful of differing viewpoints and experiences
- Gracefully accepting constructive criticism
- Focusing on what is best for the community
- Showing empathy towards other community members

Examples of unacceptable behavior:

- Harassment, intimidation, or discrimination in any form
- Offensive or unwelcome comments (related to identity, experience, etc.)
- Trolling, insulting, or demeaning remarks
- Publishing others' private information without explicit permission
- Other conduct that could reasonably be considered harassment

---

## Enforcement Responsibilities

Project maintainers have the right to remove or edit comments, commits, code,
issues, and other contributions that are not aligned with this Code of Conduct.
They may also ban contributors temporarily or permanently for repeated or
egregious violations.

---

## Enforcement

Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be
reported by contacting the project team at **hello@witchdaddylabs.com**.
All complaints will be reviewed and investigated promptly and fairly.

---

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within all project spaces — repositories, issues,
pull requests, Discord (when established), and any other official channels.

---

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org),
version 2.1, available at https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html

---

**Enforcement Guidelines** — See the [Contributor Covenant Enforcement
guidelines](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/enforcement/guidelines/2.1/).
